source.book: 《9分达人雅思听力真题还原及解析7》

segments.text: In today's lecture, I'm going to talk about digital technology, especially the changes occurring due to the rapid spread of digital technology in the last decade of the 21st century.
segments.translation: 今天的讲座中，我要谈谈数字技术，尤其是21世纪最后十年里，数字技术迅速普及所带来的变化。

segments.text: First, what is digital technology?
segments.translation: 首先，什么是数字技术？

segments.text: Well, digital technology includes any computer related devices like mobile phones, the Internet, email, instant messaging, and so on.
segments.translation: 数字技术包括任何与计算机相关的设备和技术，例如手机、互联网、电子邮件、即时通信等。

segments.text: This lecture is based on the opinions of Marc Prensky and what he believes are the main effects that high exposure to digital technology has had on young people today.
segments.translation: 本次讲座以马克·普伦斯基的观点为基础，讨论他所认为的高度接触数字技术给当今年轻人带来的主要影响。

segments.text: Prensky believes that because today's young people have been born into a digital world and spent a long time simply playing with technology, they have changed in fundamental ways.
segments.translation: 普伦斯基认为，当今的年轻人出生在数字世界中，又长期接触和使用技术，因此他们已经发生了根本性的变化。

segments.text: He thinks they are evolving differently and, as a result, processing information differently from the last generations.
segments.translation: 他认为，他们的进化方式有所不同，因此处理信息的方式也与前几代人不同。

segments.text: These young people's brains may have physically changed, although no clear evidence exists.
segments.translation: 这些年轻人的大脑甚至可能已经发生了生理变化，尽管目前还没有明确证据。

segments.text: Prensky believes that we humans can be divided into digital natives and digital immigrants.
segments.translation: 普伦斯基认为，人类可以分为数字原住民和数字移民。

segments.text: Of course, today's young people belong to the group of digital natives because they were born into this new digital age and grew up just as native speakers of the digital language of computer technology.
segments.translation: 当然，当今的年轻人属于数字原住民，因为他们出生于这个崭新的数字时代，在成长过程中就像以计算机技术的数字语言为母语的人一样。

segments.text: However, digital immigrants are the people born in the generations before the digital age.
segments.translation: 而数字移民则是出生于数字时代之前几代的人。

segments.text: When people learn a second language, they often retain their foreign accent.
segments.translation: 人们学习第二语言时，往往会保留自己的外国口音。

segments.text: It is the same with digital immigrants.
segments.translation: 数字移民也是如此。

segments.text: They aren't quite as effective at speaking the digital language as the natives are.
segments.translation: 他们使用数字语言时，不像数字原住民那样熟练自如。

segments.text: Prensky illustrates this in detail.
segments.translation: 普伦斯基对此进行了详细说明。

segments.text: For example, instead of using the Internet as a primary source of information, digital immigrants prefer finding phone numbers with a phone book or looking up information in an encyclopaedia.
segments.translation: 例如，数字移民不会把互联网作为主要信息来源，而更喜欢用电话簿查找电话号码，或从百科全书中查阅信息。

segments.text: Prensky refers to this as the digital accent.
segments.translation: 普伦斯基将这种现象称为“数字口音”。

segments.text: Another example of the digital accent is scanning a manual for a computer program, but the program itself will teach you how to use it.
segments.translation: 数字口音的另一个例子是翻看计算机程序的使用手册，尽管程序本身就会教你如何使用。

segments.text: People with a digital accent always rely on their original non-digital means of sourcing information.
segments.translation: 带有数字口音的人总是依赖原有的非数字方式来获取信息。

segments.text: They prefer doing things as they've always done rather than typing something into a computer.
segments.translation: 他们更喜欢沿用一贯的做法，而不是把内容输入计算机。

segments.text: Due to this, Prensky predicts it will bring many changes, especially in education.
segments.translation: 基于这一点，普伦斯基预测这将带来许多变化，尤其是在教育领域。

segments.text: Other educators and theorists, such as Thompson Allen, Samuel James, and Peter Vander, also give their opinions.
segments.translation: 汤普森·艾伦、塞缪尔·詹姆斯和彼得·范德等其他教育家和理论家也提出了自己的看法。

segments.text: Samuel James is a famous educator from The University of Sydney.
segments.translation: 塞缪尔·詹姆斯是悉尼大学的一位著名教育家。

segments.text: He agrees with Prensky's idea that today's educators are no longer thriving in how they teach.
segments.translation: 他赞同普伦斯基的观点，即当今教育者的教学方式已无法取得良好成效。

segments.text: However, some more traditional theorists like Peter Vander and Thompson Allen don't agree.
segments.translation: 不过，彼得·范德和汤普森·艾伦等较为传统的理论家并不认同。

segments.text: They criticize many of Prensky's assertions.
segments.translation: 他们批评了普伦斯基的许多论断。

segments.text: Vander argues that a typical classroom is much more different from what Prensky believes, with students from various backgrounds.
segments.translation: 范德认为，典型课堂与普伦斯基所设想的情况大不相同，学生有着各种不同的背景。

segments.text: He believes that many of these students are not necessarily proficient with technology.
segments.translation: 他认为，这些学生中有许多人未必熟练掌握技术。

segments.text: Then, Allen adds his opinion that even though most students in modern times can access computers at home or school easily, some don't find the digital medium appealing.
segments.translation: 接着，艾伦补充了自己的观点：尽管当今大多数学生都能在家中或学校轻松使用计算机，但有些人并不觉得数字媒介有吸引力。

segments.text: However, James disagrees.
segments.translation: 然而，詹姆斯并不同意。

segments.text: He argues that all today's students share the same basic interesting knowledge of digital technology.
segments.translation: 他认为，当今所有学生都对数字技术抱有同样的基本兴趣，并掌握相关知识。

segments.text: However, even so, James believes that our younger students can communicate with their digital immigrant teachers and still learn with the teaching methods that were quite effective in the past.
segments.translation: 不过即便如此，詹姆斯仍认为，年纪较小的学生能够与身为数字移民的老师交流，也仍然可以通过过去颇为有效的教学方法学习。

segments.text: Allen believes that both digital natives and digital immigrants have to process a large amount of information in today's digital world.
segments.translation: 艾伦认为，在当今的数字世界中，数字原住民和数字移民都必须处理大量信息。

segments.text: He thinks that although most young students today are good at playing computer games or surfing the Internet, they use computers in important ways.
segments.translation: 他认为，尽管如今大多数年轻学生擅长玩电脑游戏或上网，但他们确实会以重要的方式使用计算机。

segments.text: They are not used to using computers at advanced levels, for example, to carry out complex information searches, which are essential for university study today.
segments.translation: 他们并不习惯在高级层面使用计算机，例如开展复杂的信息检索，而这种能力对当今的大学学习至关重要。

segments.text: Unlike Allen's research, James believes that computer games can play an important role in the classroom.
segments.translation: 与艾伦的研究观点不同，詹姆斯认为电脑游戏可以在课堂中发挥重要作用。

segments.text: For example, teachers can combine interesting computer games with the lessons to attract students' attention.
segments.translation: 例如，教师可以将有趣的电脑游戏与课程结合起来，以吸引学生的注意力。

segments.text: However, Vander has different opinions.
segments.translation: 然而，范德持有不同看法。

segments.text: He argues that teachers are always the leading part of the classroom, and no computer program comes close to doing what a human teacher does every day.
segments.translation: 他认为，教师始终是课堂的主导者，任何计算机程序都远远无法媲美人类教师每天所做的工作。

segments.text: As a result, we should think of better ways to assist teachers rather than focusing on developing games.
segments.translation: 因此，我们应当思考如何更好地辅助教师，而不是专注于开发游戏。
